rules: No global tech-line overlay on body slides.
No decorative line may cross title, body, proof, chart, or image-label safe areas.
Prefer texture, light, and soft depth over repeated linework.
Linework must reveal a semantic path, map, orbit, timeline, process, or proof relationship.
If linework is only atmosphere, keep it outside the main reading corridor.
Do not stack grid, streak, orbit, connector, and particle systems on the same slide.
Cinematic linework belongs mainly on cover, divider, and closing slides.
Body slides fall back to quiet unless the line itself is the proof object.
Motion paths count as decoration unless they directly guide the reading order.

preview_rejection_signals: background lines are noticed before the title
more than one unrelated line direction competes on a body slide
decorative lines cross text, chart labels, or proof objects
the same hard diagonal or stripe system repeats across consecutive slides
lowering opacity is the only proposed fix
